content: Firms calculate the reproducibility index (RI) for software patent claim construction opinions by having multiple independent experts apply the same claim interpretation methodology to a representative sample of claims and then measuring the proportion of consistent, matching constructions, with an RI of 0.7 indicating at least 70% agreement across experts. This process often involves statistical analysis of inter-rater reliability metrics such as Cohen’s kappa or Fleiss’ kappa adapted to the claim construction context.
